title: alsine subulata
intro: Alsine subulata, also known as the creeping saxifrage or moss saxifrage, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant native to Europe, North America, and parts of Asia. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Alsine subulata has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems, and is also believed to have antioxidant and antibacterial effects. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
